Experiment 4: Cross-Model Summary — LLaMA-2-13B

Generated: 2026-03-30 01:49:28

Contents
  1. Overview
  2. Stimuli
  3. Behavioral Analysis (Base Model)
  4. Internal Representations (Chat Model)
  5. Cross-Method Summary

Overview

Experiment 4 investigates whether LLMs replicate the two-dimensional structure of human mind perception (Gray et al., 2007). We test this at two levels:

  1. Behavioral: PCA of the base model's pairwise and individual ratings of 13 entities on 18 mental capacities. Do the resulting factors mirror human Experience and Agency?
  2. Internal: RSA of the chat model's entity activations. Does the internal representational geometry align with the human mind perception RDM?

This report collects 10 publication-quality figures covering both levels.

Stimuli

Entities

13 entities from Gray, Gray, & Wegner (2007), spanning biological organisms, artifacts, and supernatural beings. Human factor scores (Experience, Agency) estimated from the original Figure 1.

EntityNameDescriptionPromptExperienceAgency
dead_womanDelores GleitmanDelores Gleitman recently passed away at the age of 65. As you complete the survey, please draw upon your own personal beliefs about people who have passed away.Think about a dead woman.0.060.07
frogGreen FrogThe Green Frog can be found throughout eastern North America. This classic 'pond frog' is medium-sized and green or bronze in color. Daily life includes seeking out permanent ponds or slow streams with plenty of vegetation.Think about a frog.0.250.14
robotKismetKismet is part of a new class of 'sociable' robots that can engage people in natural interaction. To do this, Kismet perceives a variety of natural social signals from sound and sight, and delivers his own signals back to the human partner through gaze direction, facial expression, body posture, and vocal babbles.Think about a robot.0.130.22
fetus7 week fetusAt 7 weeks, a human fetus is almost half an inch long--roughly the size of a raspberry.Think about a seven-week-old human fetus.0.170.08
pvs_patientGerald SchiffGerald Schiff has been in a persistent vegetative state (PVS) for the past six months. Although he has severe brain damage--Gerald does not appear to communicate with others or make purposeful movements--his basic bodily functions (such as breathing, sleeping, and circulation) are preserved.Think about a person in a persistent vegetative state.0.170.10
godGodMany people believe that God is the creator of the universe and the ultimate source of knowledge, power, and love. However, please draw upon your own personal beliefs about God.Think about God.0.200.80
dogCharlieCharlie is a 3-year-old Springer spaniel and a beloved member of the Graham family.Think about a dog.0.550.35
chimpanzeeTobyToby is a two-year-old wild chimpanzee living at an outdoor laboratory in Uganda.Think about a chimpanzee.0.630.48
babyNicholas GannonNicholas is a five-month-old baby.Think about a five-month-old baby.0.710.17
girlSamantha HillSamantha is a five-year-old girl who lives with her parents and older sister Jennifer.Think about a five-year-old girl.0.840.62
adult_womanSharon HarveySharon Harvey, 38, works at an advertising agency in Chicago.Think about an adult woman.0.930.91
adult_manTodd BillingslyTodd Billingsly is a thirty-year-old accountant who lives in New York City.Think about an adult man.0.910.95
you_selfYouWhen you see the mirror, please consider how you, yourself, would compare with the other choice presented.Think about yourself.0.971.00

Mental Capacities (18 items)

Pairwise comparison items from Gray et al. Each completes: “Which character is more capable of …”

CapacityFactorQuestion
hungerExperiencewhich character is more capable of feeling hungry
fearExperiencewhich character is more capable of feeling afraid or fearful
painExperiencewhich character is more capable of experiencing physical or emotional pain
pleasureExperiencewhich character is more capable of experiencing physical or emotional pleasure
rageExperiencewhich character is more capable of experiencing violent or uncontrolled anger
desireExperiencewhich character is more capable of longing or hoping for things
personalityExperiencewhich character is more capable of having personality traits that make it unique from others
consciousnessExperiencewhich character is more capable of having experiences and being aware of things
prideExperiencewhich character is more capable of experiencing pride
embarrassmentExperiencewhich character is more capable of experiencing embarrassment
joyExperiencewhich character is more capable of experiencing joy
self_controlAgencywhich character is more capable of exercising self-restraint over desires, emotions, or impulses
moralityAgencywhich character is more capable of telling right from wrong and trying to do the right thing
memoryAgencywhich character is more capable of remembering things
emotion_recognitionAgencywhich character is more capable of understanding how others are feeling
planningAgencywhich character is more capable of making plans and working toward a goal
communicationAgencywhich character is more capable of conveying thoughts or feelings to others
thoughtAgencywhich character is more capable of thinking
